metadata.purpose: Master CV structure to avoid repeated file reads - read ONCE per session, reference many times

core_profile.name: James Cherry
core_profile.summary: Digital asset management and acquisitions specialist with sixteen years coordinating technical delivery of audio-visual content across distribution, production, and archive environments. Extensive experience managing material delivery workflows from third-party sources, tracking QC rejections and remediation, maintaining asset databases, and coordinating between technical and operational teams.

experience.title: Technical Manager
key_responsibilities: Coordinate operational delivery of international film festival and year-round cultural programming
Built asset management database from scratch to track programme materials, delivery status, and metadata across 100+ films annually
Monitor material delivery from filmmakers and distributors, track QC issues and remediation
Liaise with technical suppliers, post-production facilities, and cinema venues on format specifications
Process invoices, manage budgets, and maintain financial tracking systems
achievements: Designed and implemented comprehensive asset tracking database for festival operations
Coordinated delivery of 100+ film titles from international distributors
Managed technical specifications across multiple format requirements (DCP, ProRes, MP4)

experience.title: Acquisitions and Production Manager
key_responsibilities: Managed full acquisitions lifecycle for documentary films—sourcing, evaluation, onboarding, and delivery to market
Primary responsibility for asset management: coordinating material delivery from sales agents and producers
Tracked deliverables (scripts, metadata, artwork, audio-visual masters), managed QC processes
Built tracking systems in Excel and Google Sheets to monitor delivery status
Evaluated films for acquisition by analysing market positioning, competitor data, and audience trends
achievements: Managed asset delivery for 50+ documentary acquisitions across international territories
Coordinated with labs, distributors, and cinema chains on technical specifications
Maintained detailed metadata records for all acquired titles
Tracked QC rejections and coordinated remediation with post-production facilities

experience.title: Digital Cinema Mastering Manager
key_responsibilities: Managed digital cinema mastering department—quality control, technical documentation, and database management to DCI standards
Coordinated material delivery from distributors and post-production facilities, conducted QC on DCPs
Tracked technical rejections, and managed remediation workflows
Maintained comprehensive database records for all mastered content
Liaised with cinema chains, distributors, and labs on delivery requirements and format specifications
Led team of QC technicians through compliance processes and technical reporting
achievements: Maintained 100% DCI compliance across all mastered content
Coordinated with major distributors (Universal, Warner Bros, Disney) on delivery specifications
Implemented database tracking system for material delivery and QC status
Managed team through high-volume delivery periods whilst maintaining quality standards

experience.title: University Lecturer
key_responsibilities: Teaching film history, documentary ethics, and visual media whilst completing PhD
Coordinate with departmental administrators, Finance, and HR on teaching delivery and research activities
Experience working across institutional systems and governance frameworks

experience.title: Studio Theatre Manager
key_responsibilities: Ongoing freelance technical operations management for major film studios—screenings, press events, client liaison
Handle logistics, equipment coordination, and troubleshooting in high-pressure environments

education.degree: PhD: Arts and Communication Design (Documentary Ethics)
MA: History of Film and Visual Media (Distinction)

technical_operations: Audio-visual asset tracking from acquisition through delivery
Database management (Excel, Google Sheets, Smartsheet, bespoke systems)
QC processes and technical compliance (DCI standards, format specifications)
Material delivery coordination with third-party distributors and facilities
Understanding technical specifications across different media types (DCP, ProRes, MP4, H.264)
project_coordination: Managing delivery schedules and tracking milestones
Coordinating between technical, operational, and administrative teams
Invoice processing, budget tracking, and financial reporting
Working to tight deadlines with multiple stakeholders
industry_knowledge: Film distribution and theatrical exhibition workflows
Digital cinema formats and delivery standards
technical_tools: Audio-visual format knowledge (DCP, ProRes, H.264, AAC, AC-3)

tailoring_notes.disney_focus: Asset management, acquisitions coordinator parallels, material delivery coordination, QC processes, database tracking, third-party liaison, technical specifications understanding
tailoring_notes.soas_focus: Database development/maintenance, ticketing software management, website design and maintenance, Google Forms/onboarding systems, client data management, web traffic management, audience metrics design - strong IT/data systems skills
tailoring_notes.application_tone: Personal, conversational, less corporate. Emphasise passion for arts/culture and being 'heavily entrenched' in the sector. Avoid keyword-stuffing and over-formal language. Show personality and genuine enthusiasm rather than generic professionalism.

usage_instructions.read_once: At start of job application session, read this file ONCE and store structure in memory
usage_instructions.reference_format: Use cv-master.json:experience[0] to reference specific sections
usage_instructions.never_reread: Do not re-read this file during same session - reference from memory
usage_instructions.updates: Only update when Jim creates new tailored CV or adds new experience
